classes.description: The application's top-level scripting object.

classes.description: A document.

properties.description: Its name.

properties.description: Has it been modified since the last save?

properties.description: Its location on disk, if it has one.

classes.description: A window.
classes.name: window

properties.description: The title of the window.
properties.name: name

properties.description: The unique identifier of the window.
properties.name: id

properties.description: The index of the window, ordered front to back.
properties.name: index

properties.description: The bounding rectangle of the window.
properties.name: bounds

properties.description: Does the window have a close button?
properties.name: closeable

properties.description: Does the window have a minimize button?
properties.name: miniaturizable

properties.description: Is the window minimized right now?
properties.name: miniaturized

properties.description: Can the window be resized?
properties.name: resizable

properties.description: Is the window visible right now?
properties.name: visible

properties.description: Does the window have a zoom button?
properties.name: zoomable

properties.description: Is the window zoomed right now?
properties.name: zoomed

properties.description: The document whose contents are displayed in the window.
properties.name: document

direct_parameter.description: the document(s) or window(s) to close.

parameters.description: Should changes be saved before closing?
parameters.name: saving

parameters.description: The file in which to save the document, if so.
parameters.name: saving in

parameters.description: Should changes be saved before quitting?
parameters.name: saving

commands.description: Return the number of elements of a particular class within an object.
commands.name: count

parameters.description: The class of objects to be counted.
parameters.name: each

parameters.description: The class of the new object.
parameters.name: new

parameters.description: The location at which to insert the object.
parameters.name: at

parameters.description: The initial contents of the object.
parameters.name: with data

parameters.description: The initial values for properties of the object.
parameters.name: with properties

commands.description: Move an object to a new location.
commands.name: move

parameters.description: The new location for the object(s).
parameters.name: to

values.description: Ask the user whether or not to save the file.
values.name: ask

classes.description: The Xcode application.
classes.name: application

properties.description: The active workspace document in Xcode.
properties.name: active workspace document

commands.description: Sets the selection to the line range of the document, and makes it visible in the graphical user interface.
commands.name: hack

parameters.description: The document to display.
parameters.name: document

parameters.description: The start line of the part of the document to display.
parameters.name: start

parameters.description: The stop line of the part of the document to display.
parameters.name: stop

commands.description: Create a new temporary debugging workspace.
commands.name: create temporary debugging workspace

classes.description: An Xcode-compatible document.
classes.name: document

properties.description: The document's path.
properties.name: path

classes.description: A document that represents a file on disk. It also provides access to the window it appears in.
classes.name: file document

classes.description: A document that represents a text file on disk. It also provides access to the window it appears in.
classes.name: text document

properties.description: The first and last character positions in the selection.
properties.name: selected character range

properties.description: The first and last paragraph positions that contain the selection.
properties.name: selected paragraph range

properties.description: The text of the text file referenced.
properties.name: text

properties.description: Should Xcode notify other apps when this document is closed?
properties.name: notifies when closing

classes.description: A document that represents a source file on disk. It also provides access to the window it appears in.
classes.name: source document

classes.description: A document that represents a workspace on disk. Workspaces are the top-level container for almost all objects and commands in Xcode.
classes.name: workspace document

properties.description: Whether the workspace document has finsished loading after being opened. Messages sent to a workspace document before it has loaded will result in errors.
properties.name: loaded

properties.description: The workspace's scheme that will be used for scheme actions.
properties.name: active scheme

properties.description: The workspace's run destination that will be used for scheme actions.
properties.name: active run destination

properties.description: The scheme action result for the last scheme action command issued to the workspace document.
properties.name: last scheme action result

properties.description: The workspace document's location on disk, if it has one.
properties.name: file

classes.description: An object describing the result of performing a scheme action command.
classes.name: scheme action result

properties.description: The unique identifier for the scheme.
properties.name: id

properties.description: Whether this scheme action has completed (sucessfully or otherwise) or not.
properties.name: completed

properties.description: Indicates the status of the scheme action.
properties.name: status

properties.description: If the result's status is "error occurred", this will be the error message; otherwise, this will be "missing value".
properties.name: error message

properties.description: If this scheme action performed a build, this will be the text of the build log.
properties.name: build log

classes.description: An issue (like an error or warning) generated by a scheme action.
classes.name: scheme action issue

properties.description: The text of the issue.
properties.name: message

properties.description: The file path where the issue occurred. This may be 'missing value' if the issue is not associated with a specific source file.
properties.name: file path

properties.description: The starting line number in the file where the issue occurred. This may be 'missing value' if the issue is not associated with a specific source file.
properties.name: starting line number

properties.description: The ending line number in the file where the issue occurred. This may be 'missing value' if the issue is not associated with a specific source file.
properties.name: ending line number

properties.description: The starting column number in the file where the issue occurred. This may be 'missing value' if the issue is not associated with a specific source file.
properties.name: starting column number

properties.description: The ending column number in the file where the issue occurred. This may be 'missing value' if the issue is not associated with a specific source file.
properties.name: ending column number

classes.description: An error generated by a build.
classes.name: build error

classes.description: A warning generated by a build.
classes.name: build warning

classes.description: A warning generated by the static analyzer.
classes.name: analyzer issue

classes.description: A failure from a test.
classes.name: test failure

classes.description: A set of parameters for building, testing, launching or distributing the products of a workspace.
classes.name: scheme

properties.description: The name of the scheme.
properties.name: name

properties.description: The unique identifier for the scheme.
properties.name: id

classes.description: An object which specifies parameters such as the device and architecture for which to perform a scheme action.
classes.name: run destination

properties.description: The name of the run destination, as displayed in Xcode's interface.
properties.name: name

properties.description: The architecture for which this run destination results in execution.
properties.name: architecture

properties.description: The identifier of the platform which this run destination targets, such as "macosx", "iphoneos", "iphonesimulator", etc .
properties.name: platform

properties.description: The physical or virtual device which this run destination targets.
properties.name: device

properties.description: If the run destination's device has a companion (e.g. a paired watch for a phone) which it will use, this is that device.
properties.name: companion device

classes.description: A device which can be used as the target for a scheme action, as part of a run destination.
classes.name: device

properties.description: The name of the device.
properties.name: name

properties.description: A stable identifier for the device, as shown in Xcode's "Devices" window.
properties.name: device identifier

properties.description: The version of the operating system installed on the device which this run destination targets.
properties.name: operating system version

properties.description: The model of device (e.g. "iPad Air") which this run destination targets.
properties.name: device model

properties.description: Whether this run destination is generic instead of representing a specific device. Most destinations are not generic, but a generic destination (such as "Any iOS Device") will be available for some platforms if no physical devices are connected.
properties.name: generic

commands.description: Invoke the "build" scheme action. This command should be sent to a workspace document. The build will be performed using the workspace document's current active scheme and active run destination. This command does not wait for the action to complete; its progress can be tracked with the returned scheme action result.
commands.name: build

commands.description: Invoke the "clean" scheme action. This command should be sent to a workspace document. The clean will be performed using the workspace document's current active scheme and active run destination. This command does not wait for the action to complete; its progress can be tracked with the returned scheme action result.
commands.name: clean

commands.description: Stop the active scheme action, if one is running. This command should be sent to a workspace document. This command does not wait for the action to stop.
commands.name: stop

commands.description: Invoke the "run" scheme action. This command should be sent to a workspace document. The run action will be performed using the workspace document's current active scheme and active run destination. This command does not wait for the action to complete; its progress can be tracked with the returned scheme action result.
commands.name: run

parameters.description: Additional command line arguments to pass to the action.
parameters.name: with command line arguments

parameters.description: Additional environment variables to set for the action.
parameters.name: with environment variables

commands.description: Invoke the "test" scheme action. This command should be sent to a workspace document. The test action will be performed using the workspace document's current active scheme and active run destination. This command does not wait for the action to complete; its progress can be tracked with the returned scheme action result.
commands.name: test

parameters.description: Additional command line arguments to pass to the action.
parameters.name: with command line arguments

parameters.description: Additional environment variables to set for the action.
parameters.name: with environment variables

commands.description: Start a new debugging session in the workspace. This command should be sent to a workspace document. This command does not wait for the action to complete.
commands.name: attach

parameters.description: The process identifier (pid) to which to attach.
parameters.name: to process identifier

parameters.description: Whether to start debugging in a suspended state.
parameters.name: suspended

commands.description: Start a debugging session using the "run" or "run without building" scheme action. This command should be sent to a workspace document. If no scheme is specified, the action will be performed using the workspace document's current active scheme. If no run destination is specified, the active run destination will be used. This command does not wait for the action to complete; its progress can be tracked with the returned scheme action result.
commands.name: debug

parameters.description: Scheme name.
parameters.name: scheme

parameters.description: Run destination specifier string (same as xcodebuild's -destination specifier).
parameters.name: run destination specifier

parameters.description: Whether to perform "run without building" rather than "run".
parameters.name: skip building

parameters.description: Additional command line arguments to pass to the action.
parameters.name: command line arguments

parameters.description: Additional environment variables to set for the action.
parameters.name: environment variables

values.description: The action has not yet started.
values.name: not yet started

values.description: The action ran but did not complete successfully.
values.name: failed

values.description: The action was not able to run due to an error.
values.name: error occurred

classes.description: A set of build settings for a target or project. Each target in a project has the same named build configurations as the project.
classes.name: build configuration

properties.description: The unique identifier for the build configuration.
properties.name: id

properties.description: The name of the build configuration.
properties.name: name

classes.description: An Xcode project. Projects represent project files on disk and are always open in the context of a workspace document.
classes.name: project

properties.description: The name of the project
properties.name: name

properties.description: The unique identifier for the project.
properties.name: id

classes.description: A setting that controls how products are built.
classes.name: build setting

properties.description: The unlocalized build setting name (e.g. DSTROOT).
properties.name: name

properties.description: A string value for the build setting.
properties.name: value

classes.description: An object that represents a resolved value for a build setting.
classes.name: resolved build setting

properties.description: The unlocalized build setting name (e.g. DSTROOT).
properties.name: name

properties.description: A string value for the build setting.
properties.name: value

classes.description: A target is a blueprint for building a product. Targets inherit build settings from their project if not overridden in the target.
classes.name: target

properties.description: The name of this target.
properties.name: name

properties.description: The unique identifier for the target.
properties.name: id

properties.description: The project that contains this target
properties.name: project
